title: Why do the dubs consistently mispronounce Seele
display_title: Why do the dubs consistently mispronounce Seele
wikitext:
[]: Why do the dubs consistently mispronounce "Seele"?             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:38 pm First of all, I'm no native speaker of German and I'm not fluent by a long shot. But I've taken enough German in college and studied enough on my own to know that it's not pronounced "Say-lay" but "zee-luh" or "zay-luh". As for proof, here's a video by glorious Einstuerzende Neubauten, a band which consists of real live Germans, pronouncing it in the latter manner. You should watch the whole thing for the music, but if you don't have the patience Herr Bargeld says it at 5:31 and throughout the rest of the song. [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ou6kN-XUh_U [/url:2n0ioptl] Maybe it's petty but this has bothered me for years. It's not hard to find out and they even do it in the much-improved Rebuild dub. Why did they have to be lazy?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:03 pm I think the Japanese dubs pronounce it "Zay-lei" or "Zay-luh" or something like that. (What? A bit of German that's actually slightly better in the Japanese dubs???) It's weird nobody bothered to check the pronunciation in the English dubs. Especially since Tiffany Grant knew German pretty well. Though come to think of it, her character wasn't in any of Seele's scenes. So she may not have been around to correct them during those recording sessions.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:19 pm I have a feeling that Americans like to pronounce foreign terms their own way -- I always get bothered when I hear Karaoke pronounced as Ka-ra-o-KI while in Japanese it is Ka-ra-o-KAY (which Chinese and Koreans pronounce correctly).

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:21 pm Don't worry, some Americans pronounce it that way too. Same with some people saying Nu-cu-lar instead of nuclear.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:29 pm I like how the Japanese dub of 2.22 gave Kaji a bunch of Enrish to speak, rather than having Asuka butcher the German language again. Now it's time for our language to be butchered in an Evangelion franchise!

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:44 pm I can't really get too hard on average people who can't pronounce foreign words. After all, why should you fault someone for not knowing something they were never taught? My Polish friend won't stop teasing me for butchering her language and I thought there were two characters in Eva named Rye and Asooka before I knew better. And apparently, it's physically impossible for an Arab to pronounce my real name, Andrew. But I never learned Polish or Japanese and the Arabs I talked to knew only marginal English. No, normal schlubs shouldn't be faulted for that ignorance. What bothers me is that an organization whose purpose in life is to dub anime failed to use proper pronunciation in a PROFESSIONAL FUCKING PRODUCTION. I mean, where was their quality control? How could they allow glaring errors like that to appear in the finished product that will bear their names for into the future?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jul 04, 2011 10:24 pm SAWgunner wrote: I mean, where was their quality control?

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 04, 2011 10:41 pm It bothers me more in Rebuild because it's dub was quite good. In NGE I felt like "Say-lay" was just icing on that cake of shame. In Rebuild, it was like watching a friend who has been clean for years suddenly relapse on dope. Unrelated: Even though I failed at creating a link to it, you should still check out that song. They're an aquired taste but Neubauten is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jul 04, 2011 11:22 pm Wait, it's not "seal"?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon Jul 04, 2011 11:34 pm symbv wrote: I have a feeling that Americans like to pronounce foreign terms their own way -- I always get bothered when I hear Karaoke pronounced as Ka-ra-o-KI while in Japanese it is Ka-ra-o-KAY (which Chinese and Koreans pronounce correctly).

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jul 05, 2011 12:10 am ^ This is what puzzled me, because pronunciation of KE as KAY is not difficult and does not seem to be too off in English. But it got pronounced as KI instead of silence -- it really bugs me as how it came into this way of pronunciation....

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 05, 2011 12:35 am CJD wrote: Wait, it's not "seal"?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jul 05, 2011 12:44 am I once saw a list of what languages are easiest for Japanese to learn. I think Italian is among the top because of the dominance of syllables instead of single consonants (which are a lot in English but even worse in Slavic languages).

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 05, 2011 12:58 am Eh, I like "ze-le." It rolls off the tongue very easily. It's also a good name to pronounce in hushed, fearful whispers. Go ahead, try it. I dare you!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:04 am symbv wrote: I once saw a list of what languages are easiest for Japanese to learn. I think Italian is among the top because of the dominance of syllables instead of single consonants (which are a lot in English but even worse in Slavic languages).

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:05 am If I'm correct, Seele is pronounced “zay-lay”. Nerv is “nayrf”. And yeah, Americans are total dumbasses when it comes to everything that's not them. I get tired of telling people the following all the damn time. 1. Karate is Japanese. Not Chinese. Godzilla is Japanese too. 2. No, Japan and China aren't the same thing. 3. Karate is pronounced “kaw-raw-tay”. 4. I don't take karate anyway, I take Tae Kwon Do. 5. Yes, there's a difference. 6. No, Tae Kwon Do is not a kind karate. That makes no fucking sense. 7. “Ching”, “chang”, and “chong” aren't even pronouncable in Japanese. 8. Yes, I know you only made me have to explain point seven because you like to piss me off. 9. The s in Paris is silent. 10. The p in psalm, and the t in tsunami aren't. 11. A tsunami never hit Chilee. It hit Chilè. 12. Don't even make me talk about Hebrew and Greek. God, I hate Americans.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:22 am ^ No wonder you live in Jupiter.... Yes, Karate is Japanese, or even more specifically, it is Okinawan. I also once did not know what Taekwondo is like until I saw it in summer olympics. But I still cannot tell the difference between karate and aikido, both are Okinawan but I don't know enough details about what separates the two-- from what I see aikido seems to be a mix of judo and karate....

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:36 am My Tae Kwon Do class also teaches a little bit of Judo and American kickboxing, so those three martial arts are the only ones I know much about. Anyway, I think that karate doesn't involve weapons or grappling. I may be wrong, though.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:37 am symbv wrote: ^ This is what puzzled me, because pronunciation of KE as KAY is not difficult and does not seem to be too off in English. But it got pronounced as KI instead of silence -- it really bugs me as how it came into this way of pronunciation....

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:43 am CJD wrote: The English .... is .... hardest to learn
